question_html:
Ampere’s circuital law states that:

  1. the line integral of the magnetic field along the boundary of the open surface is equal to {tex}\mu_0{/tex} times the total current passing near the surface.
  2. the line integral of the magnetic field along the boundary of the open surface is equal to {tex}\mu_0{/tex} times the total current passing through the surface.
  3. the surface integral of the magnetic field over the open surface is equal to {tex}\mu_0{/tex} times the total current passing through the surface.
  4. the surface integral of the magnetic field over the open surface is equal to {tex}\mu_0{/tex} times the total current passing near the surface.
